findings.title: Azure OpenAI Standard PAYG 30K TPM insufficient for 75 concurrent clinical agents
findings.description: 30K TPM = 500 tokens/sec. 75 concurrent agents with 4K token prompts = 300K token burst demand (10x allocated). HTTP 429 throttling is deterministic during peak.
findings.recommendation: Increase to 200K+ TPM or add APIM retry/circuit-breaker + secondary regional deployment as overflow.

findings.title: No KEDA scaling rules defined — event-driven Container Apps will not auto-scale
findings.description: Container Apps Consumption defaults to HTTP scaling. Agent runtime consumes from Event Hubs/Service Bus (not HTTP). Without KEDA triggers, agent stays at 1 replica.
findings.recommendation: Add KEDA azure-eventhubs and azure-servicebus scale rules. Set minReplicas=2 for cold-start elimination.

findings.title: Event Hubs 4 partitions caps concurrent consumers at 4 per consumer group
findings.description: With max 10 replicas but only 4 partitions, 6 replicas sit idle. Bottleneck at 4x throughput.
findings.recommendation: Increase partitions to 32 for patient-events (immutable after creation). 8-16 for others.

findings.title: Composite SLA for 99.9% target not validated
findings.description: Serial chain of 5 components at 99.9% yields 99.5%. No compensation defined.
findings.recommendation: Add composite SLA calculation section with retry/circuit-breaker compensation.

findings.title: No container image bootstrap strategy — apps will fail on first deployment
findings.description: ACR is empty when compute.bicep first runs. Container Apps will enter Failed state (ImagePullBackOff equivalent).
findings.recommendation: Use placeholder image for initial deployment. Update via CI/CD after image push.

findings.title: Cosmos DB 4000 RU/s autoscale ceiling unvalidated for peak load
findings.description: 75 agents × 10-50 RU per write = 750-3750 RU/s sustained. Burst during multi-hospital events could saturate 4000 ceiling.
findings.recommendation: Raise autoscale max to 10,000-40,000 RU/s for prod. Add 80% consumption alert.

findings.title: No readiness or liveness probes defined for Container Apps
findings.description: Deadlocked agents remain in rotation receiving events, causing silent failures.
findings.recommendation: Define liveness and readiness probes (HTTP GET /health/live and /health/ready).

findings.title: No rollback strategy for partial phased deployment failure
findings.description: Partial failures leave system in inconsistent state. No cleanup mechanism documented.
findings.recommendation: Add rollback subsection per phase. Consider deployment stacks for atomic management.

findings.title: Single-region architecture — no documented recovery path for region outage
findings.description: Acceptable v1 trade-off but should be explicitly acknowledged with future-state design.
findings.recommendation: Add DR Considerations note. Plan geo-failover for Step 7 documentation.

findings.title: Storage Account blob backup not specified for patient-data container
findings.description: ZRS protects against zone failure, not accidental deletion or corruption.
findings.recommendation: Add Azure Backup for Blobs with 7-day operational backup. Enable soft-delete and versioning.

compact_for_parent: Pass 2 (architecture-reliability) | 2 must_fix, 6 should_fix, 2 suggestions. Critical: OpenAI 30K TPM grossly insufficient for 75 concurrent agents (needs 200K+); No KEDA rules means event agents won't auto-scale beyond 1 replica. Also: EH 4 partitions caps consumers at 4; composite SLA unvalidated; no bootstrap image strategy; Cosmos 4000 RU/s ceiling unvalidated; no health probes; no rollback strategy.
